meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't debug code
content.seo_description: Learn how to master SaaS pricing and overcome debugging hurdles. A beginner-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first project.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just code; it's a logic gap often exacerbated by unclear project scope. By integrating a robust SaaS pricing strategy, you force yourself to define clear feature boundaries. Use Claude Code to inspect state and Antigravity to simulate user flow, turning vague errors into actionable business requirements.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen filled with red error text, the cursor blinking in sync with your rising anxiety. It is the classic 'Student's Wall': the code won't run, the logic feels like a labyrinth, and the dream of launching your first SaaS feels lightyears away. You aren't just stuck; you are missing the architectural map that turns a chaotic project into a structured, profitable machine.

Take a breath. The secret isn't more hours of guessing—it's shifting your perspective from 'fixing bugs' to 'building value.' By anchoring your development in a concrete SaaS pricing model, you constrain your scope and clarify your architecture. Let's transform your frustration into a production-ready roadmap.

steps.title: Define Your Value Tiers
steps.description: Before writing code, map out three pricing tiers. This forces you to define what is a 'core feature' versus 'premium,' instantly simplifying your code architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project scope and propose three SaaS pricing tiers that define feature separation for a student-level MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a schema for a Stripe-integrated pricing model based on these three tiers'

steps.title: Isolate the Debugging Logic
steps.description: Use your pricing tiers to identify which feature is failing. If the error is in a premium feature, you know exactly where to isolate the code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit my codebase for logic errors within the subscription-gated features identified in step 1.

steps.title: Simulate User Journeys
steps.description: Use tools to simulate the user experience from sign-up to payment, ensuring the pricing logic doesn't break the application flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite that simulates a user upgrading from the free tier to the pro tier, specifically looking for state management bugs.

steps.title: Implement Defensive Coding
steps.description: Refactor your error handling to ensure that if a payment call fails, the user is gracefully redirected rather than seeing a crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my current checkout function to include robust error handling and user-friendly feedback loops.

steps.title: Final Deployment Readiness
steps.description: Verify your pricing logic is production-ready and that your debugging traces are removed to ensure a clean, performant launch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a final audit of the billing integration and provide a summary of potential edge-case bugs.

pro_tips: Always build your pricing logic as a middleware service to keep it decoupled from your UI code.
Use 'Feature Flags' to toggle premium features so you can debug the base app without the complexity of payment gateways.
When you hit a bug, ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind the logic failure, not just the fix.
Document your pricing constraints in a README file; if a feature isn't in a tier, delete the code.
Maintain a separate testing environment that mirrors your production pricing variables to prevent live-data corruption.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing logic is stable, deploy your MVP to a staging environment and run a smoke test to ensure your subscription flows are airtight. From there, begin your first beta launch with a small group of users.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to turn your complex, buggy code into a professional SaaS product. Don't let technical hurdles stop your momentum. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
